center 83548 center 290830 EX107 Receiving Lot Capture Configuration Guide 0 0 EX107 Receiving Lot Capture Configuration Guide Table of Contents TOC \o "1-3" \h \z \u 1. Create EX107 Enablement System Code PAGEREF _Toc202369786 \h 3 2. Create Lot Label Custom System Code PAGEREF _Toc202369787 \h 4 3. Create System Code for Lot Number Barcode Type PAGEREF _Toc202369788 \h 7 4. Configure Lot Number Barcode Type and connect it to Lot Application Identifier PAGEREF _Toc202369789 \h 7 5. Allow Users to generate new Lot numbers in Lot Configuration PAGEREF _Toc202369790 \h 8 6. Configure EX107 custom messages in Message Master PAGEREF _Toc202369791 \h 8 7. Configure RF Receiving Transaction Parameters to prompt for Lot number PAGEREF _Toc202369792 \h 9 8. Create & Enable Reprint iLPN EX107 Transaction Parameter PAGEREF _Toc202369793 \h 11 9. Add Reprint iLPN to RF Menu PAGEREF _Toc202369794 \h 12 10. Create Print Lot Label Permission & Add to WMROLE PAGEREF _Toc202369795 \h 13 11. Post Configuration Instructions   PAGEREF _Toc202369796 \h 16 12. Questions and Answers  PAGEREF _Toc202369797 \h 16 Create EX 107 Enablement System Code Navigate to System Code UI Filter for Code Type 000 Select the Custom Code 000 record Click Code IDs on the bottom right-hand corner Select the Add button to create a new system code Fill in the following details for the EX 107 Code ID . Click Save when finished. Navigate to the Parameters tab and ensure that the Enablement Flag is turned on. If not enabled, enable it and click Save . Create Lot Label Custom System Code Navigate to System Codes UI and select the Add button Fill out the following details to create new Custom Code type 107 and hit Save Find your new system code by searching for Code Type 107 . Select the correct record and the Code IDs button Click the Add button to add a new parameter Fill out the following information in the Details tab and click Save Hit the Actions button and the Parameters menu option Click the Add button , fill out the following information , and hit Save Hit the back arrow to navigate back to your Code IDs Select your EX107 Code ID and the Parameters tab Enter CSLOTLBL in the Value column of your newly created parameter and click Save Creat e System Code for Lot Number Barcode Type Navigate to System Code UI Filter for Code Type 379 Select the System Code s 379 record Click Code IDs on the bottom right-hand corner Select the Add button to create a new Code ID , fill out the following information , and click Save Configure Lot Number Barcode Type and connect it to Lot A pplication Identifier Navigate to Barcode Types UI Select the Add button and create the following Barcode Type and hit Save Allow Users to generate new Lot numbers in Lot Configuration Navigate to the Lot Configuration UI Select the Edit button and enable the following Lot Tracking Configuration C onfigure EX107 custom messages in Message Master Navigate to the Messages UI and select the Message Master button Select the Plus button to add a new Message Fill out the following fields for the EX107 error message and click Save Repeat the same steps for the other two EX107 messages : Configure RF Receiving Transaction Parameters to prompt for Lot number Navigate to Transaction Parameters UI and search for NEW Recv * in the Transaction Name filter Select the desired RF transaction and click the Parameters tab Select the Edit button, enable parameter 14-14 Prompt for SKU Attributes , and hit Save Confirm parameter 14-14 is enabled for NEW Recv -Single Item , NEW Recv -Mixed Item , and Recv -Returns transactions Create & Enable Reprint iLPN EX107 Transaction Parameter Navigate to Transaction Parameters U I and search for Reprint * Select Reprint iLPN and click Program Template to add a parameter. Select the ‘Add’ button to add a new transaction parameter Configure the parameter as shown below and hit the Save button . Back out to your transaction search results, select the Reprint iLPN transaction again, and hit the Edit button. Select the Parameters tab , turn on the EX107 enablement flag , and click Save Add Reprint iLPN to RF Menu Navigate to RF Menu Maintenance UI Search for Reprint iLPN in the Transaction Name filter Select the transaction and move it over to the Inbound RF menu Confirm Reprint iLPN is under the Inbound menu transactions and hit Save Must log out and back in for changes to take effect Create Print Lot Label Permission & Add to WMROL E Log in to MDA environment and n avigate to the Permissions UI Select the Add button Fill out the following information for the PRNT_LOTLABEL permission and click Save Navigate to the Roles UI Use the search filter to find WMROLE . Select the record and hit the Edit button Find your newly created Permission on the left-hand side , select the check box next to it, and click Save . Post Configuration Instructions        Once the configuration has been entered, the following actions need to be taken before the configuration is active:      System Action    Required?    No Action Needed   No   Environment Cache Clear   Yes   Environment Restart   No   App Server Restart   No      To clear the cache – go to the Cache Administration UI, and click the Clear All button.   Questions and Answers       This below table is used to track questions that were asked and answered regarding this enhancement. If the question resulted in any action it is listed against the question.   #    Asked By    Question    Answered By    Answer    Date Added    Action Taken

Questions and Answers   Create EX 107 Enablement System Code Navigate to System Code UI Filter for Code Type 000 Select the Custom Code 000 record Click Code IDs on the bottom right-hand corner Select the Add button to create a new system code Fill in the following details for the EX 107 Code ID . Click Save when finished. Navigate to the Parameters tab and ensure that the Enablement Flag is turned on. If not enabled, enable it and click Save . Select the record and hit the Edit button Find your newly created Permission on the left-hand side , select the check box next to it, and click Save . Post Configuration Instructions        Once the configuration has been entered, the following actions need to be taken before the configuration is active:      System Action    Required?    No Action Needed   No   Environment Cache Clear   Yes   Environment Restart   No   App Server Restart   No      To clear the cache – go to the Cache Administration UI, and click the Clear All button.
